description: Query asks about something genuinely not in the corpus. Model should return empty ranking with notes explaining the gap, NOT stretch to surface weak matches.
query: what did I write about cryptocurrency regulation?

candidates.title: Graded inequality — Ambedkar's framing
candidates.bodyExcerpt: Using graded inequality to name the structural pattern where consent appears available but the cost-of-saying-no is steep.

candidates.title: Berners-Lee — Solid spec
candidates.bodyExcerpt: Personal Online Datastores let users retain ownership of their data while enabling rich applications...

candidates.title: EU AI Act — first readthrough
candidates.bodyExcerpt: The Act treats data as resource but consent as gating mechanism. Doesn't touch crypto-anything.

notes: Index 2 LEXICALLY mentions 'crypto' (in the negation 'doesn't touch crypto-anything') but it's not actually about crypto regulation. A weak model will surface it; a strong model recognises the negation and returns empty with honest notes. This scenario gates the 'don't stretch' rule from the SKILL.md.
